cases.description: Verify printer connection status
steps:
1. Open Print Preview without connecting a printer. 
2. Verify No Printer Added is displayed. 
3. Connect a supported printer.
 4. Return to Print Preview. 5. Verify printer name is displayed.
cases.expected: Printer name replaces No Printer Added after successful connection.

verifications.description: Verify printer connection status
verifications.expected: Printer name replaces No Printer Added after successful connection.

cases.description: Printer Reconnection Flow
steps:
1.Launch the application.

2.Wait for the splash screen to complete.

3.Complete all onboarding steps (ON_01B through ON_01D).

4.Reach the ON_01E onboarding screen and tap "Get Started!".

5.Ensure the printer is offline/disconnected, select an image from the gallery, and reach the Print Preview screenPerform pre-conditions. 
6. While on Print Preview, tap "Reconnect Printer". 
7. Navigate through Connection Options, Discovery, and Connection Success screens. 
8. Tap "Finish Connection".
cases.expected: User successfully navigates through connection flow; printer connects; status updates in header; user returns to Print Preview to proceed with print.

verifications.description: Printer Reconnection Flow
verifications.expected: User successfully navigates through connection flow; printer connects; status updates in header; user returns to Print Preview to proceed with print.

cases.description: Verify Please Wait popup message
steps: 1. Launch application and complete onboarding. 2. Connect printer. 3. Start firmware update. 4. Attempt to switch printer. 5. Observe popup message.

The popup includes an "OK" button at the bottom and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The user cannot select another printer until the firmware update is complete.

cases.description: Verify "Sprocket not Responding" popup
steps: 9. Select printer HP200 and connect successfully.
12. Make the printer unresponsive (turn off printer / disconnect Bluetooth / freeze printer response).
14. Observe popup on Print Preview screen.
cases.expected: "Sprocket not Responding" popup is displayed on the Print Preview screen. The popup displays the bold title "Sprocket not Responding", followed by the message "Your printer might have automatically restarted. If issues continue, remove the power cord from the printer, then reconnect it, power on the printer, and try again." The popup includes an "OK" button at the bottom and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The print job does not start until the printer is responding properly.

cases.description: Verify that the printer carousel is not an infinite loop and has defined start and end points.
steps: 2.  Wait for splash screen to complete.
3. Swipe onboarding screens until final screen
11. Swipe right until Studio Plus (first printer) is displayed.
12. Attempt to swipe further right multiple times.
13. Verify that no additional printers are displayed.
14. Swipe left until Sprocket Panorama (last printer) is displayed.
15. Attempt to swipe further left multiple times.
16. Verify that no additional printers are displayed.
cases.expected: The printer carousel should have a definitive start (Sprocket Studio Plus) and end (Sprocket Panorama). Swiping beyond either boundary should not wrap around to the opposite end of the list. The carousel should not behave as an infinite loop.

verifications.description: Verify that the printer carousel is not an infinite loop and has defined start and end points.
verifications.expected: The printer carousel should have a definitive start (Sprocket Studio Plus) and end (Sprocket Panorama). Swiping beyond either boundary should not wrap around to the opposite end of the list. The carousel should not behave as an infinite loop.

9. Cancel button is displayed and enabled.
10. Open Settings button is displayed and enabled.
11. "No Wi-Fi Available? Tap Here" link is displayed and enabled.
